explanation: This command uses the -s flag to execute a script from a suspicious location (C:\Users\Public\) as defined in the Sigma rule. It matches the rule's detection criteria for script mode execution from a potentially suspicious path.

This command uses the -s flag to execute a script from a suspicious location (C:\Windows\Temp\) as defined in the Sigma rule. It matches the rule's detection criteria for script mode execution from a potentially suspicious path.

This command uses the -s flag to execute a script from a suspicious location (C:\Users\Public\) without quotes. It matches the rule's detection criteria for script mode execution from a potentially suspicious path.

This command uses the -s flag to execute a script from a suspicious location (C:\ProgramData\) as defined in the Sigma rule. It matches the rule's detection criteria for script mode execution from a potentially suspicious path.
